title: Secondary platform and distribution rollout family defined
objective: Define the macOS, Windows, iOS, Android, glasses, and ambient secondary-platform release tasks that may execute ONLY after the complete Ubuntu release gate (RELEASE-002) proof is accepted, each preserving identical LifeOS contracts (the EXPERIENCE-001 governed application/state/command/event/plugin/policy/data APIs), the identical STORE-001 store-authority model (PostgreSQL + RuVector canonical durable macro-state, redb transient high-frequency tier, AgentDB per-agent cognition, envctl forward-only bridge, and the device-local lifeos.db SQLite cache that never becomes a second durable authority), and identical proof authority (every platform release recomputes to passing child proofs from the proof ledger under the RELEASE-001 north-star acceptance model, fail-closed on any capability loss, owner conflict, or paper completion). Every secondary-platform task carries the eight required gates - platform parity, signing, packaging, migration, rollback, privacy, accessibility, and provenance - plus a hard precondition edge on the accepted Ubuntu proof. This family only decomposes and gates the rollout; it performs no secondary-platform build, store submission, device deployment, or release publication (blocked_paths honored), consistent with the definition-only, fail-closed policy proven in RELEASE-001 acceptance and the archive-first charter of PRESERVE-001 / FOUNDATION-003.
inputs: RELEASE-002 (Ubuntu-first reproducible build/install/rollback family; the first release gate whose accepted proof is the precondition for every task in this family)
RELEASE-001 (North Star system acceptance model: coverage recomputed from proofs, fail-closed unless the sum equals the North Star - the proof-authority contract this family inherits unchanged)

EXPERIENCE-002 (desktop/mobile/glasses/ambient client families: bounded capabilities, shared state contract, authentication, privacy, accessibility, offline behavior, notification authority, test matrix, rollback, proof)
EXPERIENCE-001 (stable Rust-native governed application/state/command/event/plugin/policy/data APIs that replaceable presentation clients consume without owning LifeOS logic)
STORE-001 (settled store-authority model; identical across every platform)

cross-platform product requirements (Tauri 2 native shell; desktop Linux/macOS/Windows plus Tauri 2 mobile iOS/Android per src/lifeos/CLAUDE.md; database-storage-foundation lifeos.db + account.json migration)

ubuntu_gate_precondition.requirement: No task in this family may enter build, package, sign, submit, deploy, or publish until proof_records/RELEASE-002.proof.json is recorded as status=pass in proof_records/proof_ledger.jsonl (the Ubuntu-first reproducible build/install/rollback gate) AND that proof is accepted under the RELEASE-001 north-star acceptance recomputation.
ubuntu_gate_precondition.why_the_first_gate_is_ubuntu: The Architecture Blueprint's portable-release strategy is Ubuntu/Linux-first: strict static compilation against x86_64-unknown-linux-musl, bundling all dependencies to eliminate /nix/store RPATH ties and produce the install-in-place prefix. RELEASE-002 proves that path (clean source build, reproducible package hash, signed provenance, isolated install, migration, runtime, rollback, disaster recovery) before any platform that cannot use musl static linking is attempted.
ubuntu_gate_precondition.fail_closed_semantics: This precondition is verified, never assumed - the same discipline STORE-001 applies to store cutover ('a class leaves redb-durable only after a green run, never by assumption'). If the RELEASE-002 proof is absent, pending, or fail, all six platform work items stay blocked; the governance gate item RELEASE-003-G owns this fail-closed check.
ubuntu_gate_precondition.current_status_at_definition_time: PENDING - proof_records/RELEASE-002.proof.json does not yet exist in the ledger (max ledger sequence 166 = STORE-001). This family therefore encodes the dependency edge and the block; it asserts no satisfied Ubuntu proof.

parity_contract.principle: Platform parity is CONTRACT parity and AUTHORITY parity, not binary parity. Secondary platforms add new replaceable presentation shells and new build/sign/package targets; they never change LifeOS business logic, store authority, or proof authority.
parity_contract.identical_lifeos_contracts: Every client consumes the same EXPERIENCE-001 governed API surfaces - application, state, command, event, plugin, policy, and data - and owns none of them. Business logic and authority stay in lifeos-core (Rust) and the Tauri shell; the Vue/native presentation layer never touches the database directly (src/lifeos/CLAUDE.md storage-foundation contract). A platform release is accepted only if all seven governed surfaces resolve identically to the Ubuntu reference.
parity_contract.identical_store_authority: STORE-001 is platform-independent: PostgreSQL + RuVector is the single canonical durable owner of relational + semantic macro-state on every platform; redb stays the transient high-frequency tier (scratchpad / WAL / BLAKE3 dedup cache / geometry engine); AgentDB (.rvf) stays per-agent non-authoritative cognition; envctl stays the forward-only redb->embed->PostgreSQL bridge and PostgreSQL->filesystem projection engine. The device-local lifeos.db (SQLite, database-storage-foundation) is a per-device durable cache that flushes forward and NEVER becomes a second durable writer for a PostgreSQL-owned class - identical on macOS, Windows, iOS, Android, and every approved device.
parity_contract.identical_proof_authority: Every secondary-platform release resolves to passing per-platform child proofs recomputed from proof_records/proof_ledger.jsonl under the RELEASE-001 acceptance model (coverage from proofs, detect missing/conflicting outcomes, verify rollback and provenance, fail closed unless the sum equals the North Star). No platform may 'paper-complete' or introduce an owner conflict.
parity_contract.known_tension_musl_is_linux_only: The blueprint's musl static target (x86_64-unknown-linux-musl) is a Linux-only portability mechanism; it cannot produce Mach-O (macOS/iOS), PE (Windows), or Android dex/ELF artifacts. Therefore each secondary platform uses its native toolchain and bundle format and CANNOT inherit the Ubuntu binary. This is why parity is asserted at the contract/authority/proof layer (identical governed APIs, identical STORE-001 model, identical proof recomputation) rather than at the binary layer, and why the Ubuntu gate must pass first.

secondary_platform_matrix.signing_authority: Apple Developer ID Application certificate; codesign --options runtime (hardened runtime); notarytool notarization + stapling
secondary_platform_matrix.not_musl: Mach-O, links system libs / WKWebView; musl static path from RELEASE-002 does not apply

secondary_platform_matrix.signing_authority: Authenticode via signtool with OV/EV code-signing certificate, RFC-3161 timestamp; SmartScreen reputation
secondary_platform_matrix.not_musl: PE, depends on WebView2 runtime; musl static path does not apply

secondary_platform_matrix.shell: Tauri 2 iOS shell (WKWebView) built via Xcode
secondary_platform_matrix.signing_authority: Apple Distribution certificate + provisioning profile; App Store Connect submission (BLOCKED here - definition only)
secondary_platform_matrix.not_musl: Mach-O for arm64 iOS; App Store review required

secondary_platform_matrix.shell: Tauri 2 Android shell (Android System WebView) built via Gradle
secondary_platform_matrix.signing_authority: Play App Signing + upload keystore; Play Console submission (BLOCKED here - definition only)
secondary_platform_matrix.not_musl: Android ELF/dex via NDK; Play review required

secondary_platform_matrix.shell: ambient LifeOS presentation client; consumes governed APIs, reads projected LifeOS state via UDS / shared state (blueprint LifeOS-UDS contract); no durable authority on device
secondary_platform_matrix.bundle: approved-device application package per OEM runtime
secondary_platform_matrix.signing_authority: OEM/device signing per the approved-device SDK; device hardware attestation
secondary_platform_matrix.app_data_path: none durable on device - state is presented, not owned (PostgreSQL remains canonical)
secondary_platform_matrix.secret_store: no raw secrets on device; keyring/attestation references only
secondary_platform_matrix.approval_precondition: device must be on the approved-device allowlist (EXPERIENCE-002 'later approved' clause) before any release task

secondary_platform_matrix.shell: ambient display/audio client; bounded read-mostly capabilities and bounded notification authority; presents one coherent LifeOS state via governed APIs
secondary_platform_matrix.signing_authority: OEM/device signing per approved-device SDK; device attestation
secondary_platform_matrix.app_data_path: none durable on device - state presented via governed APIs; PostgreSQL canonical
secondary_platform_matrix.secret_store: no raw secrets on device; keyring/attestation references only
secondary_platform_matrix.approval_precondition: device on approved-device allowlist; notification authority bounded per EXPERIENCE-002

gate_contract.note: Each of the six platform work items (RELEASE-003-A..F) carries all eight gates below with platform-concrete values; the governance item RELEASE-003-G owns the Ubuntu-proof precondition and the proof-authority recomputation that binds them.
gates: platform_parity - identical EXPERIENCE-001 governed APIs + identical STORE-001 store authority; presentation shell replaceable, business logic/authority outside it; parity is contract/authority parity, not binary parity
signing - platform-native code-signing authority; signing secrets from the OS keyring only (STORE-001 external-secret class), never in redb or PostgreSQL
packaging - platform-native reproducible bundle with a recorded package hash; native toolchain (Mach-O/PE/Android), not musl
migration - identical lifeos.db + account.json migration and db_health/db_migrate behavior; PostgreSQL macro-state stays canonical; no store reclassified
rollback - prior signed artifact + phased-rollout halt + data rollback via PostgreSQL PITR; honors family rollback_plan; no deployment performed
privacy - platform permission model + FS scope locked to the lifeos app-data dir + secrets in keyring only
accessibility - platform a11y API parity + the axe suite (32 assertions, 0 violations) held identical to the Ubuntu reference
provenance - signed provenance/SBOM + source pins + SHA-256 content identity + SHAKE256 witness-chain lineage rooted in PostgreSQL source vectors

work_items.surface: secondary-rollout precondition and proof-authority gate (Ubuntu-proof dependency; gate: 'every secondary platform task depends on accepted Ubuntu proof')
work_items.owner: Governance Board / Release Agent / QA Agent
work_items.decision: Own the fail-closed precondition for the whole family: no platform task (A..F) may build, package, sign, submit, deploy, or publish until proof_records/RELEASE-002.proof.json is status=pass in proof_records/proof_ledger.jsonl AND accepted by the RELEASE-001 north-star recomputation. Encode the dependency edge on every platform item (depends_on_accepted_ubuntu_proof) and the proof-authority contract: each platform release recomputes to passing per-platform child proofs from the ledger, fail-closed on capability loss, owner conflict, or paper completion. Also enforce the approved-device allowlist precondition for E and F. Verified, never assumed (STORE-001 cutover discipline). At definition time RELEASE-002 proof is PENDING, so this gate holds all six platform items blocked.

gates.platform_parity: Defines the parity acceptance oracle: a platform release passes only if all seven EXPERIENCE-001 governed surfaces and the STORE-001 authority model resolve identically to the Ubuntu reference.
gates.signing: Defines that signing secrets for every platform come from the OS keyring / secret manager and never enter redb or PostgreSQL (STORE-001 external-secret/provenance class); no secret path in blocked secrets/**.
gates.packaging: Defines the reproducible-package-hash requirement inherited from RELEASE-002 and re-expressed per native toolchain for each platform.
gates.migration: Defines that the lifeos.db + account.json migration contract (database-storage-foundation) is identical across platforms and that PostgreSQL macro-state authority is never reclassified by any platform.
gates.rollback: Defines the family rollback_plan as the terminal fallback: restore the archived secondary rollout plan; no build, submission, or deployment occurs.
gates.privacy: Defines the shared privacy floor: FS scope locked to the lifeos app-data dir, secrets in keyring only, no raw secrets on any approved device.
gates.accessibility: Defines that the axe suite (32 assertions, 0 violations) and the shared accessibility model are held identical to the Ubuntu reference on every platform.
gates.provenance: Defines the provenance/proof-authority chain: signed provenance + SBOM + SHA-256 identity + SHAKE256 witness lineage rooted in PostgreSQL, recomputed under RELEASE-001 acceptance.

work_items.surface: macOS desktop release task (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin universal2; Tauri 2 WKWebView; .app + .dmg)
work_items.decision: Define the macOS release task gated on the accepted Ubuntu proof, presenting identical LifeOS contracts and store/proof authority. Definition only; no build, notarization, or distribution is performed (blocked_paths honored).

gates.platform_parity: Same seven EXPERIENCE-001 governed APIs (application/state/command/event/plugin/policy/data) consumed by a replaceable Tauri WKWebView shell; identical STORE-001 model (PostgreSQL canonical, redb transient, AgentDB cognition, envctl bridge, lifeos.db device cache never a second durable writer). Parity is contract/authority parity: the macOS binary is Mach-O universal2, not the Ubuntu musl binary.
gates.signing: Apple Developer ID Application certificate; codesign with hardened runtime (--options runtime); notarytool notarization + stapling. Signing identity from macOS Keychain (Security framework, service 'lifeos'); never in redb/PostgreSQL.
gates.packaging: Tauri macOS bundle -> .app + .dmg with a recorded reproducible package hash; native aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin toolchain, WKWebView; NOT musl static.
gates.migration: lifeos.db at ~/Library/Application Support/lifeos/lifeos.db; account.json one-time migration to the accounts table then rename to account.json.migrated-<RFC3339>; db_health/db_migrate identical to Ubuntu; PostgreSQL macro-state stays canonical.
gates.rollback: Revert to the prior notarized .dmg + pin the prior updater feed; data rollback via PostgreSQL PITR (redb transient by definition); no deployment in this task; family rollback_plan is the terminal fallback.
gates.privacy: macOS TCC permission prompts + App Sandbox entitlements; FS scope locked to ~/Library/Application Support/lifeos/*; secrets only in Keychain; no raw secrets persisted to any durable store.
gates.accessibility: VoiceOver / NSAccessibility parity; the axe suite (32 assertions, 0 violations) held identical; Lexend + JetBrains Mono with system fallback; one brand mark / one brand glow contracts preserved.
gates.provenance: Signed provenance + SBOM + source pins + SHA-256 content identity of the .dmg; SHAKE256 witness-chain lineage rooted in the PostgreSQL source vectors; recomputed under RELEASE-001 acceptance.

work_items.surface: Windows desktop release task (x86_64/aarch64-pc-windows-msvc; Tauri 2 WebView2; .msi / NSIS .exe)
work_items.decision: Define the Windows release task gated on the accepted Ubuntu proof, presenting identical LifeOS contracts and store/proof authority. Definition only; no build, signing, or distribution is performed (blocked_paths honored).

gates.platform_parity: Same seven EXPERIENCE-001 governed APIs consumed by a replaceable Tauri WebView2 shell; identical STORE-001 model and lifeos.db-never-a-second-durable-writer invariant. Parity is contract/authority parity: the Windows binary is PE + WebView2, not the Ubuntu musl binary.
gates.signing: Authenticode via signtool with an OV/EV code-signing certificate + RFC-3161 timestamp; SmartScreen reputation build-up. Signing identity from Windows Credential Manager / DPAPI (service 'lifeos'); never in redb/PostgreSQL.
gates.packaging: Tauri Windows bundle -> .msi (WiX) and/or NSIS .exe with a recorded reproducible package hash; native msvc toolchain, WebView2 runtime dependency; NOT musl static.
gates.migration: lifeos.db at %APPDATA%\lifeos\lifeos.db; account.json migration identical; db_health/db_migrate identical; PostgreSQL macro-state stays canonical.
gates.rollback: MSI transactional rollback + prior-version pin + updater feed pin; data rollback via PostgreSQL PITR; no deployment in this task; family rollback_plan terminal fallback.
gates.privacy: Windows capability/permission model; FS scope locked to %APPDATA%\lifeos\*; secrets only in Credential Manager/DPAPI; no raw secrets in durable stores.
gates.accessibility: Narrator / UI Automation parity; axe suite (32 assertions, 0 violations) identical; font + contrast contracts preserved.
gates.provenance: Authenticode signature + SBOM + source pins + SHA-256 identity of the .msi; SHAKE256 witness lineage rooted in PostgreSQL; recomputed under RELEASE-001 acceptance.

work_items.surface: iOS mobile release task (aarch64-apple-ios; Tauri 2 iOS WKWebView; .ipa via Xcode; App Store)
work_items.decision: Define the iOS release task gated on the accepted Ubuntu proof, presenting identical LifeOS contracts and store/proof authority. Definition only; no build, signing, or App Store submission is performed (blocked_paths honored).

gates.platform_parity: Same seven EXPERIENCE-001 governed APIs consumed by a replaceable Tauri iOS shell; bounded mobile capabilities per EXPERIENCE-002; identical STORE-001 model; lifeos.db in the app container is a device cache only. Parity is contract/authority parity: Mach-O arm64 iOS build.
gates.signing: Apple Distribution certificate + provisioning profile via App Store Connect. Signing identity from iOS Keychain; never in redb/PostgreSQL. Submission BLOCKED (definition only).
gates.packaging: Tauri iOS -> .ipa via Xcode with a recorded package hash; App Store review pipeline defined but not executed.
gates.migration: lifeos.db in the app container Application Support; account.json migration identical; db_health/db_migrate identical; PostgreSQL macro-state canonical.
gates.rollback: App Store Connect phased-release halt + revert to the prior approved build; data rollback via PostgreSQL PITR; no submission in this task; family rollback_plan terminal fallback.
gates.privacy: iOS privacy manifest (PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy) + App Tracking Transparency + runtime permission prompts; scoped app-container storage; secrets only in Keychain; no raw secrets in durable stores.
gates.accessibility: VoiceOver / UIAccessibility + Dynamic Type parity; axe suite equivalent (32 assertions, 0 violations) on the web layer; shared accessibility model held identical.
gates.provenance: App Store provenance + SBOM + source pins + SHA-256 identity of the .ipa; SHAKE256 witness lineage rooted in PostgreSQL; recomputed under RELEASE-001 acceptance.

work_items.surface: Android mobile release task (aarch64/armv7/x86_64-linux-android; Tauri 2 Android WebView; .aab via Gradle; Play)
work_items.decision: Define the Android release task gated on the accepted Ubuntu proof, presenting identical LifeOS contracts and store/proof authority. Definition only; no build, signing, or Play submission is performed (blocked_paths honored).

gates.platform_parity: Same seven EXPERIENCE-001 governed APIs consumed by a replaceable Tauri Android shell; bounded mobile capabilities per EXPERIENCE-002; identical STORE-001 model; lifeos.db in internal storage is a device cache only. Parity is contract/authority parity: Android ELF via NDK.
gates.signing: Play App Signing + upload keystore. Keystore/secret material from the Android Keystore / OS secret store; never in redb/PostgreSQL. Submission BLOCKED (definition only).
gates.packaging: Tauri Android -> .aab (and .apk) via Gradle with a recorded package hash; Play review pipeline defined but not executed.
gates.migration: lifeos.db in scoped internal storage; account.json migration identical; db_health/db_migrate identical; PostgreSQL macro-state canonical.
gates.rollback: Play staged-rollout halt + revert to the prior versionCode; data rollback via PostgreSQL PITR; no submission in this task; family rollback_plan terminal fallback.
gates.privacy: Android runtime permissions + Data Safety declaration + scoped storage; secrets only in the Android Keystore; no raw secrets in durable stores.
gates.accessibility: TalkBack / AccessibilityService + font scaling parity; axe suite equivalent (32 assertions, 0 violations) on the web layer; shared accessibility model held identical.
gates.provenance: Play provenance + SBOM + source pins + SHA-256 identity of the .aab; SHAKE256 witness lineage rooted in PostgreSQL; recomputed under RELEASE-001 acceptance.

work_items.surface: glasses approved-device release task (ambient LifeOS presentation client; UDS / governed-API state; allowlist-gated)
work_items.decision: Define the glasses release task gated on BOTH the accepted Ubuntu proof AND the approved-device allowlist (EXPERIENCE-002 'later approved' clause), presenting identical LifeOS contracts and store/proof authority with no durable authority on the device. Definition only; no build or device deployment is performed (blocked_paths honored).

gates.platform_parity: Presents the same LifeOS state via the EXPERIENCE-001 governed APIs and the blueprint LifeOS-UDS / shared-state read path (LifeOS reads the same memory-mapped redb state / governed API the agents write); bounded read-mostly capabilities per EXPERIENCE-002; PostgreSQL stays the canonical durable owner - the device owns no durable authority.
gates.signing: OEM/device signing per the approved-device SDK + device hardware attestation; no raw secrets on the device (keyring/attestation references only), never in redb/PostgreSQL.
gates.packaging: Approved-device application package per the OEM runtime with a recorded package hash; allowlist-gated; native device toolchain (not musl).
gates.migration: No local durable migration - the device presents projected state, it does not own lifeos.db macro-state; PostgreSQL remains canonical and unmodified by the device.
gates.rollback: Device app rollback to the prior approved image; no deployment in this task; data authority unaffected because the device is non-durable; family rollback_plan terminal fallback.
gates.privacy: Always-on sensor (camera/mic) minimization + on-device processing constraints + minimal bounded capability; no raw secrets on device; FS/state access mediated by governed APIs only.
gates.accessibility: Audio-first / caption accessibility + reduced-motion; shared accessibility model held identical to the Ubuntu reference where surfaces overlap.
gates.provenance: Device attestation + SBOM + source pins + SHA-256 identity of the device package; SHAKE256 witness lineage rooted in PostgreSQL; recomputed under RELEASE-001 acceptance.

work_items.surface: ambient approved-device release task (display/audio client; bounded capabilities + bounded notification authority; allowlist-gated)
work_items.decision: Define the ambient client release task gated on BOTH the accepted Ubuntu proof AND the approved-device allowlist, presenting one coherent LifeOS state with bounded capabilities and bounded notification authority and no durable authority on the device. Definition only; no build or device deployment is performed (blocked_paths honored).

gates.platform_parity: Presents one coherent LifeOS state via the EXPERIENCE-001 governed APIs; bounded read-mostly capabilities and bounded notification authority per EXPERIENCE-002; identical STORE-001 model; the device owns no durable authority (PostgreSQL canonical).
gates.signing: OEM/device signing per the approved-device SDK + device attestation; no raw secrets on device; secret material stays in the keyring, never in redb/PostgreSQL.
gates.packaging: Approved-device application package per the runtime with a recorded package hash; allowlist-gated; native device toolchain (not musl).
gates.migration: No local durable migration - the client presents projected state; PostgreSQL macro-state remains canonical and unmodified by the device.
gates.rollback: Ambient app rollback to the prior approved image + notification-authority revocation; no deployment in this task; family rollback_plan terminal fallback.
gates.privacy: Minimal always-on capability + bounded notification authority + on-device presentation only; no raw secrets on device; state access mediated by governed APIs.
gates.accessibility: Ambient/audio accessibility + reduced-motion + high-contrast presentation; shared accessibility model held identical where surfaces overlap.
gates.provenance: Device attestation + SBOM + source pins + SHA-256 identity of the device package; SHAKE256 witness lineage rooted in PostgreSQL; recomputed under RELEASE-001 acceptance.

store_001_alignment: This family reclassifies no store on any platform. STORE-001 is identical everywhere: PostgreSQL + RuVector is the single canonical durable owner of relational + semantic macro-state; redb stays the transient high-frequency tier (scratchpad / WAL / BLAKE3 dedup cache / geometry engine); AgentDB (.rvf) stays per-agent non-authoritative cognition; envctl stays the forward-only bridge and projection engine; external secrets stay in the OS keyring. The device-local lifeos.db (SQLite, database-storage-foundation) is a per-device durable cache that flushes forward and never becomes a second durable writer for a PostgreSQL-owned class. Adding a macOS/Windows/iOS/Android/approved-device presentation shell changes only the replaceable client and its build/sign/package target; the durable authority, the semantic authority, the cognition owner, and the secret owner are untouched.

rollback_contract.mechanism: Per-platform: prior signed/approved artifact revert + phased-rollout halt (App Store Connect / Play Console / updater feed / MSI transaction) + data rollback via PostgreSQL PITR (redb transient by definition). Family: restore the archived secondary rollout plan.
rollback_contract.rollback_plan: Restore the archived secondary rollout plan; no build, submission, or deployment occurs.
rollback_contract.guarantee: Because this family is definition-only and blocked_paths forbid secondary-platform build, store submission, device deployment, and release publication, the prior state is always restorable and no platform can strand data - durable authority remains PostgreSQL PITR-recoverable on every platform.

completion_gate: Every secondary-platform task (macOS, Windows, iOS, Android, glasses, ambient) declares a hard dependency on the accepted Ubuntu proof (RELEASE-002) and carries all eight gates - platform parity, signing, packaging, migration, rollback, privacy, accessibility, provenance - with platform-concrete values; the STORE-001 store-authority model and the EXPERIENCE-001 governed contracts are identical across platforms; proof authority is the RELEASE-001 fail-closed recomputation; and no build, submission, or deployment is performed.
